title: Chewy's Growth Is on Autopilot: The Power of Its $9.4 Billion Autoship Customer Sales
text: Chewy has built a powerful subscription-based business through its Autoship program, which accounts for nearly 80% of revenue. The pet retailer generated $11.9 billion in fiscal 2024 revenue, with $9.4 billion (79.2%) coming from customers participating in the Autoship program. This subscription service delivers pet essentials—food, medications, and litter—on regular schedules directly to customers' homes. The recurring revenue model provides Chewy with more predictable sales patterns, increased customer loyalty and retention, higher spending per customer, and ability to pass costs to subscribers more easily. CEO Sumit Singh noted that the program delivers predictable subscription-like recurring revenue streams, making Chewy's growth feel automated rather than dependent on individual purchasing decisions. Autoship participation has expanded from approximately 70% of sales in fiscal 2021 to nearly 80% today, demonstrating both the program's success and potential ceiling for expansion within Chewy's customer base. Autoship customer sales have grown more quickly than overall revenue in each of the last four years, indicating the company's ability to deepen customer relationships.
